conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Shopping for home internet -- Xfinity, AT&T Fiber, and a local co-op are all available at my new place.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Xfinity is the cheapest upfront but the reviews mention the upload speeds being pretty asymmetric.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The local co-op is appealing to support locally but their max speed tier is lower than the other two.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to unpack the last few boxes from the move still.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: AT&T Fiber offers symmetric upload and download speeds, which actually matters since I do a lot of streaming and video calls for work.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Finally unpacked the last boxes, found a box of old cables I don't even need anymore.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Tested AT&T Fiber at a friend's place who has it and the upload speed during a video call was noticeably better than my old Xfinity connection.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The co-op's pricing is attractive but the speed tier probably wouldn't cut it for what I need.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My new neighbor stopped by to introduce themselves, seem friendly.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Double-checked AT&T Fiber's coverage map and my new place is confirmed within their fiber footprint.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Going with AT&T Fiber for the new place. The symmetric upload speeds for streaming and video calls mattered more than the co-op's lower price or Xfinity's asymmetric speeds.

query: Which internet provider did the user choose for their new place?
